title: rubus × obtusicaulis
intro: Rubus × obtusicaulis, also known as the hybrid black raspberry or thimbleberry, is a deciduous shrub in the family Rosaceae, resulting from the cross between the black raspberry (Rubus occidentalis) and the thimbleberry (Rubus parviflorus). This plant contains anthocyanins, powerful antioxidants with potential medicinal properties, such as anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The health benefits of Rubus × obtusicaulis include its potential to reduce inflammation, improve cardiovascular health, and provide protection against certain types of cancer. Herbal preparations of Rubus × obtusicaulis can be made into teas, tinctures, and infusions, and are often used to support overall health and well-being.
